tldr: Affected versions of this package validate the URL host in the GraphQL asset mutation using checks that can be bypassed (e.g. via redirects or IPv6-only hostnames). A crafted URL or redirect can cause the server to request cloud metadata endpoints or internal addresses and return the response as an asset. In setups with GraphQL asset write permissions enabled, an attacker can obtain cloud credentials or probe internal services. The patch tightens host resolution and validation so that blocklisted destinations (link-local, metadata IPs, and their IPv6 equivalents) are consistently rejected.
doest_this_affect_me: You are affected if you are using a version that falls within the vulnerable range.
how_to_fix: Upgrade the `craftcms/cms` library to the patch version.
